title: poa nemoralis subsp. montana
intro: Poa nemoralis subsp. montana, also known as mountain meadow grass, is a subspecies of the wood meadow-grass, a perennial grass native to the mountainous regions of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating various health issues. The health benefits of Poa nemoralis subsp. montana include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
